Orders and Order Properties
Orders are trade instructions. Orders specify what traders want to trade, whether to buy or sell,
how much, when and how to trade, and most importantly, on what terms. Trad
rule_text: A trader should specify order parameters with precision before execution, because order submission strategy is the most important determinant of trading success and the proper order at the right time determines outcome quality.
entry_conditions: Trading intent is clearly defined (direction, size, time horizon)
Market conditions assessed for liquidity before order design
Limit price set with 3+ tick buffer from current mid-price in favor of entry
exit_conditions: Order unfilled after 50% of intended trade duration elapsed
Market moves 5+ ticks against intended entry

Trading is a zero-sum game in an important accounting sense. In a zero-sum game, the total
gains of the winners are exactly equal to the total losses of the losers. Trading is a zero-sum
game because
rule_text: A trader should only initiate trades when they have identified a counterparty motivation that suggests the counterparty will lose, because profitable trading requires trading against losers in a zero-sum game.

Price action contradicts fundamental trends (suggests forced trading, not informed trading)
exit_conditions: Exit when counterparty motivation no longer evident

they widely shop a large block buy order, and fall when they widely shop a large block s
rule_text: A trader should trade in the direction of widely-shopped large block orders, because price moves predictably follow block exposure—upward for buy blocks, downward for sell blocks—creating a momentum opportunity before front runners fully extract the benefit.
entry_conditions: Large block order is publicly visible or widely shopped across multiple venues
Price has moved at least 20 basis points in the direction of block (buy block = price up, sell block = price down)
Order size >= 500 contracts or $5M notional equivalent
Volume spike in direction of block >= 150% of 20-day average volume
exit_conditions: Exit after 50 bps profit
Exit if order flow reverses and volume moves against block direction
Exit after 30 minutes if no further progress

raw_chunk_text:
The Price Accelerator
Increases in prices transfer wealth from pessimistic traders who have short positions to optimistic
traders who have long positions. These transfers can cause accelerate price ch
rule_text: A trader should enter long positions during price accelerations driven by forced buying (margin calls and wealth-induced purchases) from both pessimistic and optimistic traders, because these cascading flows create directional momentum beyond fundamental value.
entry_conditions: Price has risen for at least 3 consecutive periods
Rate of price change is accelerating (current_move > previous_move by at least 15%)
Volume or open interest increases confirm forced buying activity
Short interest has visibly increased (pessimist positions are large)
exit_conditions: Acceleration flattens (rate of change decreases below entry threshold)
Price closes below 2-period moving average
Forced buyers have liquidated (short covering volume normalizes)
